summary: Verified new Auto-Rebracket custom parameters feature: 3 new fields (rebracket_cooldown, rebracket_lookback, rebracket_buffer) work correctly in both backend API and frontend UI. All previous features (loss logs, history filters, trade expansion) still functioning.
seed_data_creation: None required. Main agent pre-configured SPY with cooldown=60, lookback=20, buffer=0.25. TSLA updated during testing to cooldown=30, lookback=15, buffer=0.15.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auto-Rebracket section now has 5 configurable inputs when enabled: Threshold, Spread, Cooldown, Lookback Ticks, Buffer. Backend uses .get() with defaults for missing values: cooldown=0, lookback=10, buffer=0.10. Tickers in MongoDB may not have these fields stored until explicitly set via PUT API.

rebracket_cooldown.frontend: Input shows 'Cooldown (s)' with min=0, max=3600, step=5

rebracket_lookback.frontend: Input shows 'Lookback Ticks' with min=2, max=100, step=1

backend_rebracket_logic.cooldown_logic: Lines 508-513: Checks rebracket cooldown before allowing rebracket
backend_rebracket_logic.lookback_logic: Lines 515-520: Uses configurable lookback for price history window
backend_rebracket_logic.buffer_logic: Line 526: Uses buffer for new_buy = recent_low - buffer

loss_logs.content: Verified log file contains all metadata fields (entry_price, target_price, avg_price, etc.)

history_tab.filters: All, Buys, Sells, Stops, Trail, Losses buttons work
history_tab.expansion: Click on trade row shows detailed metadata (Fill Price, Quantity, Total Value, Buy Power, Target Price, Avg Price)
